mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that compresses the equity discount rate and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of easing months.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5 percent signals a deeply impaired labor market that crushes consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the triggered signal Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad contraction in aggregate demand and manufacturing output that amplifies downside earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month, or the joint signal Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, a month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals a marginal labor market recovery that boosts consumption and equity risk appetite more than in normal times, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE > 8%, month-over-month PAYEMS increases fail to produce positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the return spread versus low-unemployment regimes is not significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO momentum coincides with positive returns in fewer than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600 thousand units signals a frozen residential construction sector that eliminates construction jobs and downstream consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 600, or the triggered signal Sharpe ratio is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL, negative real interest rates force capital reallocation from fixed income into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where FEDFUNDS is below CPIAUCSL inflation fail to produce positive SPX 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to coincide with positive SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.
